events.title: D'mt Kingdom Flourishes
events.description: Pre-Aksumite civilization centered at Yeha with ties to South Arabia.

events.title: Rise of Aksum
events.description: Aksum emerges as a major Red Sea power, noted in the Periplus of the Erythraean Sea.

events.title: Frumentius Shipwrecked
events.description: Syrian Christians Frumentius and Aedesius survive a shipwreck and enter the royal court.

events.title: Conversion of Aksum
events.description: King Ezana officially embraces Christianity; Frumentius consecrated bishop by Athanasius.

events.title: Conquest of Meroe
events.description: Aksum under Ezana conquers the declining kingdom of Meroe (Kush).

events.title: Arrival of the Nine Saints
events.description: Syrian monks flee Byzantine persecution, translate the Bible, and found monasteries in Ethiopia.

events.title: Najran Massacre
events.description: Jewish king Dhu Nuwas massacres Christians in Najran, sparking international outrage.

events.title: Kaleb’s Expedition
events.description: Aksumite King Kaleb conquers Yemen to protect persecuted Christians.

events.title: First Hijra
events.description: Aksumite king gives refuge to early Muslims fleeing Mecca.

events.title: Inland Shift of Aksum
events.description: Due to Islamic expansion and environmental degradation, the political center moves south to Lasta/Amhara.
